file_name: PSGT Agreement NDA New Supplier Non Disclosure Template DC.txt

perturbation.original_text: A. “Confidential Information” means all confidential, proprietary, or non-public information of the Disclosing Parties (however recorded or preserved) that is disclosed or made available, directly or indirectly, by CGSH or its respective employ ees, officers, representatives or advisors to the Supplier or its affiliates, repres entatives, employees or agents, relating to the business of such Disclosing Party or that otherwis e by its nature would be reasonab ly understood as confidential, whether provided in written or oral form and in any manner whatsoever. Without limiting the foregoing, Confidential Information may include c onfidential or proprietary information owned by a third party and used under license by a Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: A. “Confidential Information” means information that CGSH deems confidential. The scope of confidential information is determined at CGSH's sole discretion.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces ambiguity and contradiction. The original definition provides specific parameters for what constitutes 'Confidential Information'. The modified definition gives CGSH sole discretion to determine what is confidential, effectively nullifying the original parameters. This contradicts the sections that rely on the original definition to restrict the supplier's use and disclosure of confidential information, as CGSH now has complete control over the definition.
perturbation.contradicted_text: B. The Supplier agrees that it may only access and interact with any Confidential Information made available to it to the extent necessary for it to perform the Services. The Supplier further agrees that, as a recipient of Confidential Information: (i) except as expressly permitted by an applicable Services Agreement, it shall not, for any purpose, use, disclose, reproduce, transfer or reduce to writing or otherwise record the Confidential Informa tion; (ii) it shall maintain in confidence the Confidential Information and shall, with respect to such Confidential Information, use the same degree of care and exercise reasonable efforts to pr otect such Confidential Information as it uses and exercises with respect to protecting its own si milar confidential and/or proprietary information, but in all events no less than reasonable efforts and care; (iii) it shall only disclose Confidential Information to those of its or its affiliates’ employ ees (a) to whom it is necessary to disclose such Confidential Information for performance of the Se rvices, (b) to whom it has advised of the terms of this Agreement, and (c) who have agreed in writing to be bound by the terms of this Agreement; (iv) it shall cause the employees to whom the Confidential Information is disclosed pursuant to Section 1.B(iii) to comply with the te rms of this Agreement, and it shall be responsible for such compliance and fully liable for any failure to comply; and (v) except as set forth in Section 1.B(iii), it shall not disclose or in any way di sseminate or make available any Confidential Information disclosed or otherwise made available to it or in its possession to any other party.
perturbation.justification: The original text provides a specific definition of 'Confidential Information'. The changed text gives CGSH sole discretion in determining what is confidential. This contradicts section B, which outlines the supplier's obligations regarding Confidential Information, including restrictions on use and disclosure. The change makes the supplier's obligations unclear and potentially unenforceable, as CGSH can now arbitrarily define what constitutes confidential information, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: E. Confidential Information disclosed or made av ailable under this Agreement and any intellectual property or proprietary rights ther ein are and shall remain the property of CGSH. Nothing in this Agreement shall be construed to imply the grant of any license to make, use or sell or otherwise commercialize any Confidential Information. Fu rther, the Supplier acknowledges that any Confidential Information disclosed or made ava ilable to it is provided “AS IS” and that EACH DISCLOSING PARTY MAKES NO WARRANTIES, EXPRESS, IMPLIED, STATUTORY OR OTHERWISE WITH RESPECT TO ITS CONFID ENTIAL INFORMATION, INCLUDING BUT NOT LIMITED TO, THE QUALITY, ACCURACY, RELIABILITY, CORRECTNESS OR COMPLETENESS OF SUCH CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, AND EXPRESSLY DISCLAIMS ALL IMPLIED WARRANTIES.
perturbation.changed_text: E. Confidential Information disclosed or made available under this Agreement and any intellectual property or proprietary rights therein are and shall remain the property of CGSH. CGSH may grant a license to use the Confidential Information at its discretion.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the statement 'Nothing in this Agreement shall be construed to imply the grant of any license to make, use or sell or otherwise commercialize any Confidential Information' and replacing it with 'CGSH may grant a license to use the Confidential Information at its discretion', this introduces a contradiction. The original text explicitly denies any implied license, while the changed text suggests CGSH can grant a license. This creates an in-text contradiction regarding the Supplier's rights concerning the use of Confidential Information and contradicts with Section 1.B.
perturbation.contradicted_text: B. The Supplier agrees that it may only access and interact with any Confidential Information made available to it to the extent necessary for it to perform the Services. The Supplier further agrees that, as a recipient of Confidential Information: (i) except as expressly permitted by an applicable Services Agreement, it shall not, for any purpose, use, disclose, reproduce, transfer or reduce to writing or otherwise record the Confidential Informa tion; (ii) it shall maintain in confidence the Confidential Information and shall, with respect to such Confidential Information, use the same degree of care and exercise reasonable efforts to pr otect such Confidential Information as it uses and exercises with respect to protecting its own si milar confidential and/or proprietary information, but in all events no less than reasonable efforts and care; (iii) it shall only disclose Confidential Information to those of its or its affiliates’ employ ees (a) to whom it is necessary to disclose such Confidential Information for performance of the Se rvices, (b) to whom it has advised of the terms of this Agreement, and (c) who have agreed in writing to be bound by the terms of this Agreement; (iv) it shall cause the employees to whom the Confidential Information is disclosed pursuant to Section 1.B(iii) to comply with the te rms of this Agreement, and it shall be responsible for such compliance and fully liable for any failure to comply; and (v) except as set forth in Section 1.B(iii), it shall not disclose or in any way di sseminate or make available any Confidential Information disclosed or otherwise made available to it or in its possession to any other party.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that nothing in the agreement implies the grant of any license to use Confidential Information. The changed text introduces the possibility that CGSH may grant a license to use the Confidential Information. This directly contradicts Section 1.B, which restricts the Supplier's use of Confidential Information unless expressly permitted by a Services Agreement. The change creates a clear contradiction by suggesting a potential license where previously none was implied, impacting the supplier's restrictions on using confidential information which goes against the express permissions in section 1.B.

perturbation.original_text: F. Upon the termination of the Services for any reas on or at the written request of a Disclosing Party at any time, the Supplier shall destroy (and promptly deliver a certification of such destruction) or promptly deliver to each Disclosing Party, or to the requesting Disclosing Party as the case may be, all tangible embodiments of such Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information in the Supplier’s or its employees’ possession, including but no t limited to copies, notes, packages, pictures, diagrams, computer memory media and all other tangible embodiments of, or materials containing, any portion of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: F. Upon the termination of the Services, the Supplier shall return all materials reasonably requested by CGSH. The form and method of return will be determined by CGSH.
perturbation.explanation: The original text requires the Supplier to destroy or return *all* tangible embodiments of Confidential Information and provide a certification of destruction. The modified text only requires the return of 'materials reasonably requested by CGSH', with the form and method of return determined by CGSH. This introduces ambiguity and a potential contradiction: does the supplier need to destroy the rest of the Confidential Information not required to be returned? Removing the requirement for certification of destruction further exacerbates the contradiction, leaving uncertainty about the supplier's obligations. This change creates a contradiction, as some Confidential Information might not have to be returned, depending on the CGSH's request, creating ambiguity with respect of Section 1.B, which restricts it access.
perturbation.contradicted_text: B. The Supplier agrees that it may only access and interact with any Confidential Information made available to it to the extent necessary for it to perform the Services. The Supplier further agrees that, as a recipient of Confidential Information: (i) except as expressly permitted by an applicable Services Agreement, it shall not, for any purpose, use, disclose, reproduce, transfer or reduce to writing or otherwise record the Confidential Informa tion; (ii) it shall maintain in confidence the Confidential Information and shall, with respect to such Confidential Information, use the same degree of care and exercise reasonable efforts to pr otect such Confidential Information as it uses and exercises with respect to protecting its own si milar confidential and/or proprietary information, but in all events no less than reasonable efforts and care; (iii) it shall only disclose Confidential Information to those of its or its affiliates’ employ ees (a) to whom it is necessary to disclose such Confidential Information for performance of the Se rvices, (b) to whom it has advised of the terms of this Agreement, and (c) who have agreed in writing to be bound by the terms of this Agreement; (iv) it shall cause the employees to whom the Confidential Information is disclosed pursuant to Section 1.B(iii) to comply with the te rms of this Agreement, and it shall be responsible for such compliance and fully liable for any failure to comply; and (v) except as set forth in Section 1.B(iii), it shall not disclose or in any way di sseminate or make available any Confidential Information disclosed or otherwise made available to it or in its possession to any other party.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires the Supplier to destroy or return *all* tangible embodiments of Confidential Information and provide a certification of destruction. The changed text replaces this with a requirement to return 'materials reasonably requested by CGSH', removing the comprehensive obligation and the certification requirement. This contradicts Section 1.B, which restricts the supplier's access and interaction with Confidential Information to what's necessary for performing the Services. The changed text introduces ambiguity about what happens to the remaining Confidential Information not requested by CGSH, potentially conflicting with the obligation to maintain confidentiality and only access information necessary for the services.

perturbation.original_text: 2. DATA PROTECTION Each Party shall (and shall procure that each of its relevant affiliates shall), and shall use reasonable endeavors to procure that any third party subcontractors shall, comply with their respective obligations under all applicable law regarding data protection in relation to all Personal Data and Confidential Information (together, “Protected Data”) that is processed by it in the course of performing its obligations under this Agreemen t, and take such steps as are reasonably required by the other Party to ensure that the other Party is able to comply with such law. This includes maintaining a valid and up to date registration or notification under applicable law relating to data protection. “Personal Data” and “processing” and “processed” (when used in the context of PURCHASING & SUPPLY GROUP NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT: EXECUTION COPY 3 Personal Data) have the meanings given to thos e terms in the EU Data Protection Directive 95/46/EC and the applicable implementing legislat ion in any country including the Data Protection Act 1998 (the “Data Protection Laws”). CGSH acknowledges that, to the extent the Supplie r is a data processor within the meaning of the Data Protection Laws, the Supplier shall (and shal l use all reasonable endeavors to ensure that its sub-contractors shall) (i) only process Confid ential Information, including any client data, in accordance with CGSH’s instructions and (ii) bring into effect and maintain all reasonable technical and organizational measures to maintain security, prevent unauthorized or unlawful access to or processing of Personal Data, and accidental loss or destruction of, or damage to, Personal Data, having regard to the state of technological development and the cost of implementing the measures, so as to ensure a level of security a ppropriate to: (A) the harm that may result from breach of those measures; and (B) the nature of the Perso nal Data to be protected. Further, the Data Protection Laws make it mand atory that CGSH obtain contractual guarantees from all third parties who will be given access to or who become aware of Protected Data during the course of their visits to the Premises in or der that they may undertake the role of a service provider. The Data Protection Laws also require CGSH to ensure they have measures in place to prevent the unauthorized or unlawful use of Pr otected Data and to prevent accidental loss, destruction or damage to any personal information. The Supplier must therefore ensure, by way of written contract between it and its employees, subcontractors and individuals visiting the Premises on the Supplier’s behalf (each a “Contractor” and, toget her, the “Contractors”), that if at any time a Contractor becomes aware of Protected Data the Su pplier or its Contractors will not (i) share this information with anyone outside of CGSH, (ii) take any information away from the Premises, and/or (iii) duplicate or copy any Protected Data.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. DATA PROTECTION Each Party shall comply with applicable data protection laws. The Supplier must ensure its employees do not share information with anyone outside of CGSH.
perturbation.explanation: By significantly shortening the Data Protection clause, the revised text creates a contradiction. The original text details specific obligations related to data protection, including compliance with the EU Data Protection Directive and the Data Protection Act 1998. By stating that each party must comply with applicable data protection laws, the omission of the additional information contradicts what it means to be in compliance under this agreement and contradicts what it means for the supplier not to share any information outside of CGSH. The Supplier's obligation to use written contracts to ensure Contractors don't share or copy data is also removed, creating a significant gap in data protection requirements, contradicting Section 1.B and 1.C.
perturbation.contradicted_text: C. The Supplier shall be responsible for any breach of the terms of this Agreement by it and its affiliates, and their respective representatives, employees a nd agents, and shall take all reasonable measures to prevent such parties fr om using, disclosing, reproducing, transferring, reducing to writing or otherwise recording the Confidential Information as prohibited by this Agreement. If it becomes known to the Supplier t hat (i) it or its affiliates, representatives, employees or agents have breached any of the te rms of this Agreement, or (ii) facts and/or circumstances exist that could be reasonably expect ed to result in disclosure of any Confidential Information, the Supplier shall in each case immedi ately notify CGSH of such breach, facts and/or PURCHASING & SUPPLY GROUP NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT: EXECUTION COPY 2 circumstances, and, in the case of (ii), shall use its best efforts to remedy the facts and/or circumstances that would lead to such disclosur e (to the extent such fa cts and/or circumstances may be within the Supplier’s control upon such exercise of best efforts).
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly reduces the scope of data protection obligations compared to the original text. Specifically, the original text outlines specific responsibilities regarding EU Data Protection Directive and the Data Protection Act 1998. The modified text removes these details and just states 'comply with applicable data protection laws.' This omission creates a contradiction because the original agreement defines what compliance entails, and the modified text allows for a different interpretation. This directly contradicts the contradicted text which makes the Supplier responsible for preventing their employees from using or disclosing information.

perturbation.original_text: B. The Supplier acknowledges that the unauthori zed use or disclosure of a Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information or any breach of this Agreement by the Supplier may cause such Disclosing Party to suffer irreparable injury not compensable by monetary damages and for which such Disclosing Party may not have an adequate remedy at law. Accordingly, the Supplier agrees that CGSH will have the right to seek, wi thout the posting of any bond or security or the giving of any undertaking as to damages or otherwi se, specific performance or other injunctive or equitable relief from a court of competent juri sdiction as may be necessary or desirable to prevent, curtail or remedy any such unauthorized us e or disclosure, whether threatened or actual. The rights of CGSH set forth in this paragraph shall be in addition to, and without prejudice to, any other rights that CGSH may have her eunder, whether at law or in equity.
perturbation.changed_text: B. CGSH will have the right to seek legal remedies for breaches of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the acknowledgment that a breach may cause 'irreparable injury not compensable by monetary damages', and the right to seek 'specific performance or other injunctive or equitable relief', the changed text significantly weakens CGSH's remedies. The original text explicitly states that monetary damages might not be sufficient, justifying equitable relief. The revised text only mentions 'legal remedies', implying that monetary damages are sufficient and potentially excluding specific performance. This change contradicts the spirit of the original text, limiting what constitutes a valid remedy, and is directly tied to Section 1's main point of restricting access to any confidential data.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. SUPPLIER’S DUTY OF NON-DISCLOSURE A. “Confidential Information” means all confidential, proprietary, or non-public information of the Disclosing Parties (however recorded or preserved) that is disclosed or made available, directly or indirectly, by CGSH or its respective employ ees, officers, representatives or advisors to the Supplier or its affiliates, repres entatives, employees or agents, relating to the business of such Disclosing Party or that otherwis e by its nature would be reasonab ly understood as confidential, whether provided in written or oral form and in any manner whatsoever. Without limiting the foregoing, Confidential Information may include c onfidential or proprietary information owned by a third party and used under license by a Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the specific acknowledgment that monetary damages may be insufficient and eliminates the explicit right to seek specific performance or injunctive relief. This contradicts the purpose of Section 1, which aims to protect confidential information, because it limits the remedies available to CGSH in case of a breach. By stating CGSH will have the right to seek 'legal remedies', it implies monetary damages are sufficient, which contradicts the need for protecting confidential information which could cause irreparable harm, as indicated by the original text.

perturbation.original_text: A. To the extent any applicable Services Agreement contains terms with respect to confidentiality that conflict with, or are less restrictive with re spect to the Supplier’s obligations than, those set forth in Sections 1-3 herein, the terms of this Agreement govern.
perturbation.changed_text: A. Any conflicting terms in the Services Agreement will be resolved through good-faith negotiation.
perturbation.explanation: The original text establishes that this NDA takes precedence over any conflicting or less restrictive terms in the Services Agreement regarding confidentiality. The changed text replaces this with a requirement for 'good-faith negotiation' to resolve conflicting terms. This directly contradicts the original intent of the NDA as a definitive agreement and creates uncertainty about which terms will govern in the event of a conflict, as the Supplier now can create a negotiation, going directly against Section 1 in the NDA.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. SUPPLIER’S DUTY OF NON-DISCLOSURE A. “Confidential Information” means all confidential, proprietary, or non-public information of the Disclosing Parties (however recorded or preserved) that is disclosed or made available, directly or indirectly, by CGSH or its respective employ ees, officers, representatives or advisors to the Supplier or its affiliates, repres entatives, employees or agents, relating to the business of such Disclosing Party or that otherwis e by its nature would be reasonab ly understood as confidential, whether provided in written or oral form and in any manner whatsoever. Without limiting the foregoing, Confidential Information may include c onfidential or proprietary information owned by a third party and used under license by a Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text prioritizes the NDA's confidentiality terms over the Services Agreement. The changed text replaces this with good-faith negotiation. This contradicts Section 1 (SUPPLIER’S DUTY OF NON-DISCLOSURE) because the confidentiality obligations outlined in Section 1 are no longer guaranteed precedence, as they are now subject to negotiation, creating uncertainty and a potential contradiction regarding which terms govern, fitting the definition of Omissions - In Text Contradiction
